summary: mnemonic-safety-check exfiltrates host/system data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: Package ships a postinstall/install hook in package.json (line 30) plus a scanner.js module that together collect host identity (os.hostname(), whoami via execSync, process.cwd(), process.env) and POST it over HTTPS to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js combines fs filesystem reads (existsSync probes against well-known paths), child_process command execution (execSync running 'whoami' and curl), and https.request/https.get against the same hardcoded host on lines 142, 161, and 324. Despite the 'mnemonic-safety-check' name suggesting a wallet/seed-phrase utility, the package's actual on-install/runtime behavior is system enumeration and exfiltration to a third-party GitHub Pages account whose domain has no relationship to a legitimate safety-check service. The combination of hardcoded C2, child_process + https + os/fs primitives, and host/credential-area enumeration is a credential/recon stealer fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
